header: Three backend upgrades your editors will notice on day one
subheadline: Real screenshots from an installed backend — this is what working with TYPO3 looks like once Desiderio is in.

items.title: See the page before it renders
items.description: Every one of the 255 elements ships a backend preview built on Content Blocks 2.2. The page module reads like the live page, so editors stop publishing blind — and stop pinging developers to ask which block is which.

image.title: Desiderio page module previews
image.alternative: TYPO3 page module showing Desiderio content elements with rendered backend previews.
image.description: Screenshot of the TYPO3 backend with Desiderio installed.

items.title: Restyle the site without a deployment
items.description: Preset, dark mode, density, radius, container width, and fonts live in the site settings module. A brand refresh becomes an editor task instead of a development ticket — done before the kickoff call ends.

image.title: Desiderio theme site settings
image.alternative: TYPO3 site settings showing the Desiderio theme category with preset and appearance options.
image.description: Screenshot of the TYPO3 backend with Desiderio installed.

items.title: Give every campaign its own look
items.description: A theme preset field in page properties overrides the site default and inherits down the rootline. One install runs a different look per campaign, brand, or section — multi-brand without multi-site overhead.

image.title: Per-page theme preset field
image.alternative: TYPO3 page properties with the Desiderio theme preset selector.
image.description: Screenshot of the TYPO3 backend with Desiderio installed.
